title: Back to school
big_question: Kể lại các trải nghiệm và hoạt động trong trường học bằng tiếng Anh như thế nào nhỉ?
grammar: Present perfect with 'already' and 'yet': Have you had some food yet? - We have already met.
Relative clauses with 'who', 'which', 'where': I’m the leopard who likes to rap. This is the jungle where I rule.
phonics: Spelling patterns for long vowel sounds: rain, day, cake
skills: Revising relative pronouns and present perfect
speaking_prompts: What lesson have we got now?
Have you had some food yet?
Istanbul is the city where...

mini_games.prompt: You throw your rubbish in here when you are at school.
You leave your bicycle here when you arrive at school.
I haven't done my homework _______.

title: The treasure
big_question: What kinds of treasure are there? (Có những loại kho báu nào trên thế giới nhỉ?)
grammar: Present perfect with 'for' and 'since': I've had these lovely spots since I was a cub. I've had my big, long tail for eleven years.
Questions with 'How long...?': How long have you had them?
phonics: Words with '-sure' and '-ture' endings: treasure, future, picture, measure
skills: Reading and speaking about finds and treasures
Listening and writing a blog entry / portfolio
project: Make a poster about gold or silver
speaking_prompts: What kinds of treasure are there?
How long have you had this?
I've had it since 2019.

mini_games.prompt: You use this tool to dig a hole in the ground.
Pirates look through these to see things far away.
I've had my bike _______ six months.

title: Future transport
big_question: How will people travel in the future? (Con người sẽ di chuyển như thế nào trong tương lai?)
grammar: Expressing necessity with 'need to' / 'don't need to': You don't need to drive a car. All you need to do is climb to get from here to there.
Making predictions with 'will' / 'won't': There will be a lot of noise. There won't be any trees left.
phonics: Pronunciation of '-sion', '-ssion', and '-tion' endings: unvoiced /ʃ/ (station) and voiced /ʒ/ (television)
skills: Listening and writing about future travel
project: Write an advertisement for a new school bus
speaking_prompts: What do you need to bring for the trip?
How will we travel in 2050?
I think there will be flying cars.

mini_games.prompt: It looks like a train, but it travels up in the air on rails.
You wear this machine on your back to fly in the air.
You _______ to take a passport to travel to another country.

title: Ancient Egypt
big_question: What was life like in Ancient Egypt? (Cuộc sống ở Ai Cập cổ đại diễn ra như thế nào?)
grammar: Past passive (was/were + past participle): The pyramids were built by slaves. Who was this den built by?
Quantifiers (a lot of / lots of / a few / a little): There were lots of trees in this jungle. I have a little money.
skills: Speaking, reading and listening about Ancient Egypt
project: Design and describe an Ancient Egyptian crown
speaking_prompts: What was life like in Ancient Egypt?
How were the pyramids built?
There were a lot of slaves.

mini_games.prompt: A king of ancient Egypt.
A dead body from ancient Egypt wrapped in bandages.
The blocks of rock _______ taken to Giza on boats.

title: Olympic sports
big_question: What do we know about sports? (Chúng ta biết những gì về các môn thể thao nhỉ?)
grammar: Possibility with 'could': We could play some volleyball. You could try to teach me.
Present continuous for future arrangements: I'm running in the morning. I'm cycling on my bike.
phonics: Four ways of pronouncing the letter 'y': funny, yellow, pyramid, sky
project: Plan and write a leaflet with training ideas for a school sports team
speaking_prompts: We could try some archery.
What are you doing on Saturday?
I'm flying to Rome on Monday.

mini_games.prompt: You use a bow and arrows in this sport.
You lift very heavy things in this Olympic sport.
We _______ go to the cinema tonight if you want.

title: In London
big_question: Why is London famous? (Tại sao Luân Đôn lại nổi tiếng nhỉ?)
grammar: Present perfect with 'ever' and 'never': Have you ever been to London Zoo? - No, she hasn't. She's never been.
Present perfect and past simple: I had a swim. I went home.
phonics: Pronunciation of 's' and 'z' sounds: shops, weeks / amazing, animals
project: Design a new building for your school
speaking_prompts: Have you ever been to London?
I've never watched a football match on TV!
Which attractions in London would you most like to visit?

mini_games.prompt: You go to this shop to buy medicine and soap.
You buy fresh bread and cakes in this shop.
Have you _______ eaten Chinese food?

title: Crazy inventions
big_question: How do inventions help us? (Làm thế nào những phát minh giúp ích cho chúng ta?)
grammar: Quantifiers with 'too many' / 'not enough': I've got too many jobs to do. I haven't got enough time.
Asking for information 'Can you tell me what this is / does / is for?': Can you tell me what the machine is? Can you tell me what this button does? Can you tell me what the machine is for?
phonics: Different ways of pronouncing the letters 'gh': high, laugh, through, caught, enough
project: Draw a toy to put in a park for younger children and explain how it works
speaking_prompts: Can you tell me what this button does?
What is the most important invention?
I've got too many jobs to do.

mini_games.prompt: You use this tool to hit nails into wood.
You use this tool for cutting wood.
I haven't got _______ time to play.

title: This is Houston
big_question: What do we know about space? (Chúng ta biết những gì về vũ trụ?)
grammar: Gerunds (verb + -ing) as subjects and objects: Going on a space trip is exciting! Staying up late is really tiring.
Reported speech: He said that he was back from the stars. He said that he didn't know the way.
phonics: Different spellings of the sound /ʌ/: sun, son, love
skills: Listening, writing and speaking about space
project: Find out about and draw a constellation and answer questions about it
speaking_prompts: Going on a space trip is exciting!
What do we know about space?
The alien said that he was from Mars.

mini_games.prompt: Special clothing worn by astronauts in space.
A large round hole in the ground on the moon.
He said _______ he wanted a map.

title: A cold place
big_question: What's it like at the Earth's poles? (Khí hậu ở các vùng cực của Trái Đất như thế nào?)
grammar: Question tags with 'be': It's cold at the poles, isn't it? And the nights are dark, aren't they?
Modals 'may' / 'might' for possibility: The jungle may be in danger. They might take our home away.
phonics: Word stress in longer words: penguin, adventure, yesterday
skills: Speaking and listening about the Arctic and Antarctic
project: Make a storyboard or a slide presentation about the Arctic
speaking_prompts: What's it like at the Earth's poles?
It's cold at the poles, isn't it?
We might go to a concert tomorrow.

mini_games.prompt: A house made of ice bricks.
A big block of ice in the sea.
It's cold today, _______ it?

title: The Jurassic Age
big_question: What do we know about prehistoric times? (Chúng ta biết những gì về thời tiền sử?)
grammar: 2nd conditional: If I had a time machine, I'd go back to the past. If I met a dinosaur, I wouldn't stop to say 'hello'.
2nd conditional questions: What would you do if you saw a dinosaur?
phonics: Different spellings of the sound /ɔː/: short, awful, ball, dinosaur
skills: Listening, reading and writing about the Stone Age
project: Design and make a stone circle on a paper plate
speaking_prompts: What do we know about prehistoric times?
What would you do if you saw a dinosaur?
If I had a time machine, I'd go to the year 2166.

mini_games.prompt: A small area of water, like a small lake.
The time of day when the sun appears in the sky.
If I _______ a time machine, I'd visit the future.
